The '''Qella''' were a quadruped [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Brath Qella]].

==History==
Genetic descendants of the [[Ahra Naffi]], the Qella discovered that their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was on a collision course with one of the orbiting [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. In preparation for what they knew to be a coming ice age, the Qella built a [[Starship/Legends|vessel]] containing their history, genetic data, and instructions for how to return to [[Brath Qella]] and restart their [[Species/Legends|species]] when the time was right. The vessel, which came to be known as the ''[[Teljkon Vagabond]]'', departed Brath Qella around [[120 BBY]] and became a myth among [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]]. The moon's impact came shortly thereafter, killing the remaining Qella.

In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], the ''Vagabond'' returned to Brath Qella and began the process of restarting Qella life. The process was aided by [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who [[Mind trick|hid]] the ''Vagabond'' from prying eyes, giving the species and their world a chance at a new beginning. [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Joto Eckels]] believed it was possible that the Qella could return anywhere from a hundred years or a thousand years later.

==Appearance==
The Qella were tall, lanky quadrupeds who had stubby three-fingered hands; oval, smooth-skinned bodies not suited for cold climates and slender double-jointed limbs.

==Technology==
The Qella were much like the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] in that their technology was grown from all the lifeforms that had existed on Brath Qella. While they didn't have the mass production capabilities as the Yuuzhan Vong had, they were able to make a much more powerful ship, the ''Teljkon Vagabond''. The major difference seems to be that the Qella were a peaceful species, only seeking to prolong their own species lives with the construction of the ''Vagabond'', and not destruction, as the ''Vagabond'' only attacked what it perceived as threats to its mission.
